Package com.github.mrgarbagegamer

package com.github.mrgarbagegamer
Provides a high-performance, brute-force solver for a hexagonal Lights Out-style puzzle.

Package Architecture

This package implements a highly optimized producer-consumer architecture designed for solving a complex combinatorial problem. The core components work together to generate, distribute, and validate potential solutions with maximum efficiency.

The primary components are:

  • Generator (CombinationGeneratorTask): A recursive ForkJoinTask that explores the solution space. It now generates compact WorkBatch.WorkItem ranges, offloading final combination assembly to the consumer.
  • Monkey (TestClickCombination): A worker thread (or "monkey") that receives batches of combinations, applies them to a grid instance, and validates the outcome.
  • Orchestrator (StartYourMonkeys): The main entry point that initializes and manages the entire system, including the ForkJoinPool for producers and the queue array for consumers.

High-Performance Concurrency and Data Structures

The solver's performance relies on several key design patterns:

  • Range-Based Batching: The WorkBatch class is central to performance. Instead of containing individual combinations, it holds WorkBatch.WorkItem objects that describe large ranges of combinations. This dramatically reduces producer overhead and queue contention.
  • Centralized Queue: The CombinationQueueArray acts as a high-performance, multi-lane queue that distributes WorkBatch objects to available consumer threads.
  • Bitmask Grid: The puzzle state is managed by the Grid class, which uses a long[2] bitmask for ultra-fast, O(1) click operations and state validation.
  • Aggressive Pooling: To minimize GC overhead, the system uses extensive object pooling, including an ArrayPool for combination arrays, a TaskPool for generator tasks, and centralized recycling of WorkBatch objects.
  • Class
    Description
    ArrayPool
    A high-performance, non-thread-safe object pool for recycling fixed-size short[] arrays.
    CombinationGeneratorTask
    A RecursiveAction that generates combinations of clicks for the Lights Out puzzle solver.
    CombinationGeneratorTask.GeneratorContext
    A container for all thread-local resources used by a generator.
    CombinationMessage
    A specialized Log4j2 Message for logging solution combinations with low overhead.
    CombinationQueue
    A thin, type-safe wrapper around a JCTools MpmcArrayQueue designed exclusively for transferring WorkBatch objects between producer and consumer threads.
    CombinationQueueArray
    A structure for managing work distribution and shared resources between generators and monkeys.
    Grid
    A structure that represents the core hexagonal grid for a "Lights Out" style puzzle.
    Grid.ValueFormat
    Defines the different formats used to represent a cell's location on the grid.
    Grid13
    A concrete Grid that provides the initial puzzle state for Q13 ("Kermit").
    Grid22
    A concrete Grid that provides the initial puzzle state for Q22 ("Shrek").
    Grid35
    A concrete Grid that provides the initial puzzle state for Q35 ("Buttercup").
    StartYourMonkeys
    The main application entry point and orchestrator for the Lights Out puzzle solver.
    TaskPool
    A non-thread-safe, high-performance object pool for recycling CombinationGeneratorTask instances.
    TestClickCombination
    An optimized worker thread that tests potential puzzle solutions from a shared work queue.
    WorkBatch
    A high-performance, reusable, and iterable container for batching puzzle combinations.
    WorkBatch.WorkItem
    A compact, reusable representation of a range of combinations that share a common prefix.

Performance-optimized brute-force solver for hexagonal grid Lights Out puzzles
